_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to explain how ideas in texts are organised using structures like taxonomies, cause and effect, extended metaphors and chronological order.

_source.w: We are learning to explain how ideas in texts are organised using structures like taxonomies, cause and effect, extended metaphors and chronological order.
y: Understanding the underlying organisation of ideas helps us comprehend complex texts more efficiently.
Recognising these structures helps us organise our own ideas more effectively.
This analytical skill is essential for academic reading and writing across all disciplines.
eg: We identify cause-and-effect structures in explanation texts and discuss how they organise ideas logically.
We analyse how a text uses taxonomy — classifying ideas into categories and subcategories.
We explain how an extended metaphor structures and unifies a text's ideas.
We compare chronological and non-chronological organisation and discuss the effects of each.
We map the organisational structure of a complex text using a graphic organiser.
We apply our understanding of these structures when planning our own extended texts.
